summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Appeals court remands Illinois card fee law
articles.description: A move by federal officials to preempt Illinois’ interchange fee ban requires further review by a district court, the 7th Circuit said Friday.

articles.title: Block leans into its AI future
articles.description: After cutting 4,000 jobs, the company is eager to demonstrate the benefits of artificial intelligence both internally and for customers.

articles.title: Fed to launch roundtable to fight payment fraud
articles.description: The Federal Reserve sees a rising threat of payment fraud and wants to collaborate with other agencies to stop it, a Fed Vice chair said.

articles.title: US agentic sales to reach $1T by 2030: report
articles.description: But even as the technology grows in popularity, the physical shopping experience will remain important, per a new report.

articles.title: Global Payments touts Genius gains
articles.description: The payments processor has moved quickly to push its Genius point-of-sale solution to Worldpay’s sales channels and financial customers.

articles.title: Fiserv moves through ‘transition year’
articles.description: During its first-quarter earnings report Tuesday, the payments processor’s CEO cited new executive hires and cost cuts aimed at reviving growth.

articles.title: PayPal CEO aims to keep company intact
articles.description: In comments to analysts, the payment player’s new leader side-stepped speculation about breaking up the company, saying he sees PayPal, Venmo and Braintree together.

articles.title: How Spirit Airlines customers can navigate payments
articles.description: One consumer advocacy group is urging the airline’s customers to seek refunds “quickly” after the carrier’s abrupt shutdown.
